destroy.description: Permanently delete the linked PowerSync Cloud instance and its data. Requires --confirm=yes.

confirm.description: Set to "yes" to confirm destruction of the instance.
confirm.name: confirm

directory.description: Directory containing PowerSync config. Defaults to "powersync". This is required if multiple powersync config files are present in subdirectories of the current working directory.
directory.name: directory

instance-id.description: PowerSync Cloud instance ID. Manually passed if the current context has not been linked.
instance-id.name: instance-id

org-id.description: Organization ID (optional). Defaults to the token’s single org when only one is available; pass explicitly if the token has multiple orgs.
org-id.name: org-id

project-id.description: Project ID. Manually passed if the current context has not been linked.
project-id.name: project-id

destroy.summary: [Cloud only] Permanently destroy the linked Cloud instance.

login.description: Store a PowerSync auth token (PAT) in secure storage so later Cloud commands run without passing a token. If secure storage is unavailable, login can optionally store it in a local config file. Use PS_ADMIN_TOKEN env var for CI or scripts instead.
login.summary: Store auth token for Cloud commands.

logout.description: Remove the stored PowerSync auth token from secure storage or local fallback config storage. Cloud commands will no longer use stored credentials until you run login again.

status.description: Fetch instance diagnostics: connection status, active and deploying sync config, replication state. Output as human-readable, JSON, or YAML. Cloud and self-hosted.

output.description: Output format: human-readable, json, or yaml.
output.name: output

api-url.description: [Self-hosted] PowerSync API URL. When set, context is treated as self-hosted (exclusive with --instance-id). Resolved: flag → cli.yaml → API_URL.
api-url.name: api-url

instance-id.description: [Cloud] PowerSync Cloud instance ID (BSON ObjectID). When set, context is treated as cloud (exclusive with --api-url). Resolved: flag → cli.yaml → INSTANCE_ID.
instance-id.name: instance-id

org-id.description: [Cloud] Organization ID (optional). Defaults to the token’s single org when only one is available; pass explicitly if the token has multiple orgs. Resolved: flag → cli.yaml → ORG_ID.
org-id.name: org-id

project-id.description: [Cloud] Project ID. Resolved: flag → cli.yaml → PROJECT_ID.
project-id.name: project-id

directory.description: Directory containing PowerSync config. Defaults to "powersync". This is required if multiple powersync config files are present in subdirectories of the current working directory.
directory.name: directory

status.summary: Show instance diagnostics (connections, sync config, replication).

stop.description: Deactivate the linked PowerSync Cloud instance. Requires --confirm=yes. Restart later with powersync deploy.

confirm.description: Set to "yes" to confirm stopping the instance.
confirm.name: confirm

project-id.name: project-id

stop.summary: [Cloud only] Stop the linked Cloud instance (restart with deploy).

validate.description: Run validation checks on local config: config schema, database connections, and sync config. Requires a linked instance. Works with Cloud and self-hosted.

output.description: Output format: human-readable, json, or yaml.
output.name: output

api-url.description: [Self-hosted] PowerSync API URL. When set, context is treated as self-hosted (exclusive with --instance-id). Resolved: flag → cli.yaml → API_URL.
api-url.name: api-url

instance-id.description: [Cloud] PowerSync Cloud instance ID (BSON ObjectID). When set, context is treated as cloud (exclusive with --api-url). Resolved: flag → cli.yaml → INSTANCE_ID.
instance-id.name: instance-id

org-id.description: [Cloud] Organization ID (optional). Defaults to the token’s single org when only one is available; pass explicitly if the token has multiple orgs. Resolved: flag → cli.yaml → ORG_ID.
org-id.name: org-id

project-id.description: [Cloud] Project ID. Resolved: flag → cli.yaml → PROJECT_ID.
project-id.name: project-id

directory.description: Directory containing PowerSync config. Defaults to "powersync". This is required if multiple powersync config files are present in subdirectories of the current working directory.
directory.name: directory

validate.summary: Validate config schema, connections, and sync config before deploy.

deploy.description:
Deploy local config (service.yaml, sync config) to the linked PowerSync Cloud instance.
Validates connections and sync config before deploying.
See also powersync deploy sync-config to deploy only sync config changes.
See also powersync deploy service-config to deploy only service config changes.

deploy-timeout.description: Seconds to wait after scheduling a deploy before timing out while polling status (default 300 seconds).
deploy-timeout.name: deploy-timeout

directory.description: Directory containing PowerSync config. Defaults to "powersync". This is required if multiple powersync config files are present in subdirectories of the current working directory.

instance-id.description: PowerSync Cloud instance ID. Manually passed if the current context has not been linked.

org-id.name: org-id

project-id.description: Project ID. Manually passed if the current context has not been linked.
project-id.name: project-id

deploy:service-config.description: Deploy only service config changes (without sync config updates).

fetch.description: Subcommands: list Cloud instances in org/project (fetch instances), print instance config as YAML/JSON (fetch config), or show instance diagnostics (fetch status).
fetch.summary: List instances, fetch config, or fetch instance diagnostics.

fetch:instances.description: List PowerSync Cloud instances, grouped by organization and project.

org-id.description: Optional Organization ID. Defaults to all organizations.
org-id.name: org-id

output-file.description: Optionally write instance information to a file.
output-file.name: output-file

project-id.description: Optional Project ID. Defaults to all projects in the org.
project-id.name: project-id

fetch:instances.summary: [Cloud only] List Cloud instances in the current org/project.

fetch:status.description: Fetch instance diagnostics: connection status, active and deploying sync config, replication state. Output as human-readable, JSON, or YAML. Cloud and self-hosted.

output.description: Output format: human-readable, json, or yaml.
output.name: output

api-url.description: [Self-hosted] PowerSync API URL. When set, context is treated as self-hosted (exclusive with --instance-id). Resolved: flag → cli.yaml → API_URL.
api-url.name: api-url

instance-id.name: instance-id

org-id.description: [Cloud] Organization ID (optional). Defaults to the token’s single org when only one is available; pass explicitly if the token has multiple orgs. Resolved: flag → cli.yaml → ORG_ID.
org-id.name: org-id

project-id.description: [Cloud] Project ID. Resolved: flag → cli.yaml → PROJECT_ID.
project-id.name: project-id

generate.description: Generate client artifacts: schema (from instance schema + sync config) or a development token for connecting clients. Cloud and self-hosted where supported.
generate.summary: Generate client schema or development token.

generate:schema.description: Generate a client-side schema file from the instance database schema and sync config. Supports multiple output types (e.g. type, dart). Requires a linked instance. Cloud and self-hosted.

output.description: Output type: dart, dotNet, flutterFlow, js, jsLegacy, kotlin, swift, ts
output.name: output

output-path.description: Path to output the schema file.
output-path.name: output-path

api-url.description: [Self-hosted] PowerSync API URL. When set, context is treated as self-hosted (exclusive with --instance-id). Resolved: flag → cli.yaml → API_URL.
api-url.name: api-url

instance-id.description: [Cloud] PowerSync Cloud instance ID (BSON ObjectID). When set, context is treated as cloud (exclusive with --api-url). Resolved: flag → cli.yaml → INSTANCE_ID.
instance-id.name: instance-id

org-id.description: [Cloud] Organization ID (optional). Defaults to the token’s single org when only one is available; pass explicitly if the token has multiple orgs. Resolved: flag → cli.yaml → ORG_ID.
org-id.name: org-id

project-id.description: [Cloud] Project ID. Resolved: flag → cli.yaml → PROJECT_ID.
project-id.name: project-id

directory.description: Directory containing PowerSync config. Defaults to "powersync". This is required if multiple powersync config files are present in subdirectories of the current working directory.
directory.name: directory

generate:schema.summary: Generate client schema file from instance schema and sync config.

generate:token.description: Generate a JWT for development clients to connect to PowerSync. Cloud: uses instance dev-token API (allow_temporary_tokens must be enabled). Self-hosted: signs with shared secret from config. Requires --subject; optional --expires-in-seconds.

expires-in-seconds.description: Expiration time in seconds. Default is 43,200 (12 hours).
expires-in-seconds.name: expires-in-seconds

kid.description: [Self-hosted only] Key ID of the key to use for signing the token. If not provided, the first key will be used.
kid.name: kid

api-url.description: [Self-hosted] PowerSync API URL. When set, context is treated as self-hosted (exclusive with --instance-id). Resolved: flag → cli.yaml → API_URL.
api-url.name: api-url

instance-id.description: [Cloud] PowerSync Cloud instance ID (BSON ObjectID). When set, context is treated as cloud (exclusive with --api-url). Resolved: flag → cli.yaml → INSTANCE_ID.
instance-id.name: instance-id

org-id.description: [Cloud] Organization ID (optional). Defaults to the token’s single org when only one is available; pass explicitly if the token has multiple orgs. Resolved: flag → cli.yaml → ORG_ID.
org-id.name: org-id

project-id.description: [Cloud] Project ID. Resolved: flag → cli.yaml → PROJECT_ID.
project-id.name: project-id

directory.description: Directory containing PowerSync config. Defaults to "powersync". This is required if multiple powersync config files are present in subdirectories of the current working directory.
directory.name: directory

generate:token.summary: Generate a development JWT for client connections.

init:cloud.description: Copy a Cloud template into a config directory (default powersync/). Edit service.yaml then run link cloud and deploy.

directory.description: Directory containing PowerSync config. Defaults to "powersync". This is required if multiple powersync config files are present in subdirectories of the current working directory.
directory.name: directory

vscode.description: Configure the workspace with .vscode settings for YAML custom tags (!env).
vscode.name: vscode

init:cloud.summary: Scaffold a PowerSync Cloud config directory from a template.

init.description: Scaffold a PowerSync config directory from a template. Use init cloud or init self-hosted. For Cloud, edit service.yaml then run link cloud and deploy.
init.summary: Scaffold a PowerSync config directory from a template.

init:self-hosted.description: Copy a self-hosted template into a config directory (default powersync/). Configure service.yaml with your self-hosted instance details.

directory.description: Directory containing PowerSync config. Defaults to "powersync". This is required if multiple powersync config files are present in subdirectories of the current working directory.
directory.name: directory

vscode.description: Configure the workspace with .vscode settings for YAML custom tags (!env).
vscode.name: vscode

init:self-hosted.summary: Scaffold a PowerSync self-hosted config directory from a template.

link:cloud.description: Write or update cli.yaml with a Cloud instance (instance-id, org-id, project-id). Use --create to create a new instance from service.yaml name/region and link it; omit --instance-id when using --create. Org ID is optional when the token has a single organization.

create.description: Create a new Cloud instance in the given org and project, then link. Do not supply --instance-id when using --create.
create.name: create

instance-id.description: PowerSync Cloud instance ID. Omit when using --create. Resolved: flag → INSTANCE_ID → cli.yaml.
instance-id.name: instance-id

org-id.description: Organization ID. Optional when the token has a single org; required when the token has multiple orgs. Resolved: flag → ORG_ID → cli.yaml.
org-id.name: org-id

project-id.description: Project ID. Resolved: flag → PROJECT_ID → cli.yaml.
project-id.name: project-id

directory.description: Directory containing PowerSync config. Defaults to "powersync". This is required if multiple powersync config files are present in subdirectories of the current working directory.
directory.name: directory

link:cloud.summary: Link to a PowerSync Cloud instance (or create one with --create).

link.description: Write cli.yaml so this directory's config is bound to a PowerSync instance. Once linked, commands use that instance without passing IDs. Use link cloud or link self-hosted.
link.summary: Bind this directory to a Cloud or self-hosted instance (writes cli.yaml).

link:self-hosted.description:
Links a self hosted PowerSync instance by API URL.
API Keys can be specified via input or specified in the PS_ADMIN_TOKEN environment variable.

api-url.description: Self-hosted PowerSync API base URL (e.g. https://powersync.example.com).
api-url.name: api-url

directory.description: Directory containing PowerSync config. Defaults to "powersync". This is required if multiple powersync config files are present in subdirectories of the current working directory.
directory.name: directory

link:self-hosted.summary: Link to a self-hosted PowerSync instance by API URL.

migrate.description: Migrate PowerSync config to newer formats. Use migrate sync-rules to convert Sync Rules to Sync Streams.
migrate.summary: Migrate config to newer formats (e.g. Sync Rules → Sync Streams).

migrate:sync-rules.description: Migrates Sync Rules to Sync Streams

input-file.description: Path to the input sync rules file. Defaults to the project sync-config.yaml file.
input-file.name: input-file

output-file.description: Path to the output sync streams file. Defaults to overwrite the input file.
output-file.name: output-file

directory.description: Directory containing PowerSync config. Defaults to "powersync". This is required if multiple powersync config files are present in subdirectories of the current working directory.
directory.name: directory

migrate:sync-rules.summary: Migrates Sync Rules to Sync Streams

pull.description: Download current config from PowerSync Cloud into local YAML files. Use pull instance; pass --instance-id and --project-id when the directory is not yet linked (--org-id is optional when the token has a single organization).
pull.summary: [Cloud only] Download Cloud config into local service.yaml and sync-config.yaml.

pull:instance.description: Fetch an existing Cloud instance by ID: create the config directory if needed, write cli.yaml, and download service.yaml and sync-config.yaml. Pass --instance-id and --project-id when the directory is not yet linked; --org-id is optional when the token has a single organization. Cloud only.

directory.description: Directory containing PowerSync config. Defaults to "powersync". This is required if multiple powersync config files are present in subdirectories of the current working directory.
directory.name: directory

instance-id.description: PowerSync Cloud instance ID. Manually passed if the current context has not been linked.
instance-id.name: instance-id

org-id.description: Organization ID (optional). Defaults to the token’s single org when only one is available; pass explicitly if the token has multiple orgs.
org-id.name: org-id

project-id.description: Project ID. Manually passed if the current context has not been linked.
project-id.name: project-id

pull:instance.summary: Pull an existing Cloud instance: link and download config into local service.yaml and sync-config.yaml.
